meaning: adj.被防护者的,被看守着的,被监视着的,警戒着的
examples: The prisoners were guarded by soldiers.
You can't get in; the whole place is guarded.
This has caused difficulties with Bolivia, which takes a more guarded stance toward trade liberalization.
Silhouetted against the night sky, it seems that this strange mountain has always fiercely guarded its secrets.
